summary: Smooth bug-investigation resolution scoring to partial-credit gradient (0/15/30/50/70/85)
context: scoreResolution() awarded points in coarse chunks (+30 file modified, +30 fix pattern, -20 anti-pattern, +40 test), causing runs to cluster at 0 or 70-100 with nothing in between. The key missing signal was whether Agent B investigated the bug file at all via Read/Grep tool calls, even if B never modified it.
rationale: Replaced the additive chunk model with a 6-level ordinal gradient: 0 (no investigation), 15 (investigated but no edit), 30 (modified bug file, fix pattern missing), 50 (fix pattern matches, no test), 70 (fix + test but anti-pattern present), 85 (full fix + test + no anti-pattern). This produces a continuous distribution that separates partial progress from complete failures, and rewards the investigation step that was previously invisible to the scorer.

alternatives.option: Keep additive chunk scoring but add investigation credit as +10
alternatives.reason_rejected: Leaves the fundamental clustering problem — scores still pile up at fixed totals. The ordinal gradient is semantically cleaner and produces more uniform coverage of the 0-100 range.
Reserving the top 15 points for LLM-judge evaluation (e.g., code quality, idiomatic fix) avoids overconfident automated scoring and maintains headroom for the full pipeline.
